Highlights: THIS... is a 1996 Suzuki Carry Truck, finished in tan with a gray interior. This Carry is a Japanese-spec, right-hand drive truck equipped with a 4-speed manual transmission and is registered in New Hampshire. Imported from Japan, it's equipped with a metric instrument cluster, and the odometer shows about 19,600 kilometers, which represents approximately 12,200 miles. Equipment includes a locking rear axle, drop-down bedsides, textured vinyl upholstery, manual windows, air conditioning, and an AM/FM radio. Modifications reported by the seller are limited to a color change from white to tan and a set of 13-inch aftermarket wheels. Suzuki introduced the Carry commercial kei truck/van platform in October 1961. The first generation was considered a semi-cabover design due to the small hood on the front of the trucklet. By the time the ninth-generation model arrived in Japan in 1991 the Carry had lost its hood but had gained a reputation as a dependable workhorse. Throughout the ninth generation, two fascias were offered, the rounded headlights denoted cheaper trucks while the rectangular headlights indicated a more well-appointed truck. Power comes from a 657cc F6A 3-cylinder engine, which produces approximately 42 horsepower and 42 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear or all four wheels through a 4-speed manual transmission.
Equipment: Locking rear axle Drop-down bedsides Textured vinyl upholstery Manual windows Air conditioning AM/FM radio
Modifications: Color change from white to tan 13-inch aftermarket wheels
Known Flaws: Some paint flakes and imperfections around exterior (shown in gallery) Dents on driver's door Dents inside bed Imperfections on tailgate Curb rash and worn finish on wheels Some rust on the bed floor and on the underbody The seller reports that the A/C system may need to be recharged.
Recent Service History: The selling dealer reports that a general inspection was performed in preparation for sale, though records are not available.
Ownership History: The seller states that they imported this Suzuki in January 2023.
Other Items Included in Sale: 1 key Import documentation
Seller Notes: Since this Suzuki is an imported vehicle, it may not pass emissions testing in some states. As always, it’s the buyer’s responsibility to perform all due diligence regarding registering this truck in their respective state prior to placing a bid. New Hampshire does not require a title for vehicles built prior to 2000, and as a result, this Carry will be sold with a bill of sale and the corresponding registration documents. Please contact the seller directly for more information. The seller reports that some areas of the chassis have been painted.

Highlights: THIS... is a 1995 Land Rover Defender 90 NAS, finished in Alpine White with a black interior. The attached Carfax history report shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this Defender's past. Factory equipment includes 16-inch alloy wheels, a dual-range transfer case, air conditioning, and manual windows, among others listed below. Modifications to this Defender 90 are extensive, and the full list is detailed below. Highlights include a 4.6 Rover V8 from a 2003 Land Rover Discovery, LED exterior lights, a Lelo Fabrication roof rack and ladder system, Puma doors, aftermarket leather upholstery, aftermarket headliner and carpet, and a Mud-UK center dashboard console. The NAS Defender 90 was sold new in North America with revisions designed to comply with U.S. market demands and regulations; accordingly, NAS Defender 90 models are legal to register throughout the United States and Canada (including California). NAS Defender 90 models were offered in the U.S. for the 1994, 1995, and 1997 model years. They differed from their rest-of-world counterparts in that they came with a V8 engine, unique bodywork, and an external roll cage. Power comes from a 4.6-liter Rover V8, rated at 217 horsepower and 300 lb-ft of torque. This engine may be making more power as a result of the modifications, though a dyno sheet is not included. Output is sent to all four wheels through a 5-speed manual transmission and a 2-speed transfer case with a locking center differential.

Highlights: THIS... is a 2002 BMW M3, finished in Titanium Silver Metallic with an Imola Red interior. This M3 features the desirable 6-speed manual transmission. The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents or mileage discrepancies in this BMW's past. Modifications to this M3 are extensive, and the full list is detailed below. The third-generation M3 made its debut for the 2000 model year as the quickest and most powerful evolution of the E46-generation 3 Series. Offered as a coupe and as a convertible, it received a high-revving straight-6 engine, suspension improvements, and a muscular-looking exterior design. More than 20 years after its introduction, the third-generation M3 remains a favorite among enthusiasts thanks in part to its excellent handling and classic BMW styling. Power comes from an S54 3.2-liter straight-6, rated at 333 horsepower and 262 lb-ft of torque when left stock. Due to the modifications performed to this M3, it may produce more power, but a dyno sheet was not provided to confirm. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

The seller reports no modifications. Audi released the C7-generation A6 for the 2012 model year. It was lighter than its predecessor, it wore a sharper design, and benefitted from a more luxurious interior largely shared with the A7. A mid-cycle facelift in 2015 gave the A6 a revised front grille, exterior lighting, bumpers, side skirts, and tailpipes, as well as an update to the MMI infotainment system. Power comes from a TDI 3.0-liter turbodiesel V6, rated at 240 horsepower and 428 lb-ft of torque. Modifications reported by the seller include 17-inch Enkei RPF1 wheels, an I.L. Motorsport coolant expansion tank, MeistarR ZetaCRD coilovers, and Mazda RX-8 front wheel hubs. The NC generation served as the longest-running model in the Miata's history, with a full decade of production between 2006 and 2015. Ushering in the first completely new design for the model, this generation saw an expansion of the Miata's appeal with the addition of a power retractable hardtop model and the new range-topping Grand Touring trim. Power comes from a 2.0-liter inline-4, rated at 170 horsepower and 140 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

The attached Carfax vehicle history report indicates no mileage discrepancies in this Grand Cherokee’s past; it also shows Southern ownership since new. As the marque's flagship model, it underwent regular powertrain and interior upgrades, and by its final evolution, it remained one of the most capable and luxurious factory off-roaders offered to the public. Power comes from a 3.0-liter turbodiesel V6, rated at 240 horsepower and 420 lb-ft of torque. Output is directed to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic transmission.

Highlights: THIS... is a 2007 Chevrolet Corvette Z06, finished in Victory Red with a black interior. This Corvette features a 6-speed manual transmission. The attached Carfax history report contains a gap between 2009 and 2013, but shows no accidents or mileage discrepancies for the given period of time. It also shows that this Corvette has been registered mostly in Nevada and California from new. This Z06's factory-fitted 7.0-liter V8 has been replaced by a Late Model Engines (LME) 7.0-liter 427 LSX short block. Other modifications reported by the seller include Mahle pistons, Manley connecting rods, a Callies crankshaft, ported and polished heads, a Brian Tooley Racing Stage 3 cam, an Omni Motorsports flex fuel kit, LG Motorsports longtube headers, 18-inch front and 19-inch rear Signature Forged SV103 wheels, an electric remote exhaust cutoff, C6 ZR1 carbon ceramic brakes, LG Motorsports G2 adjustable coilovers, and more as detailed below. Factory equipment includes a limited-slip differential, carbon fiber fenders, dual zone automatic climate control, a head-up display, and more as listed below. Released for 2005, the C6 Corvette kept its predecessor's proportions but featured an updated exterior design with exposed headlights returning to the range for the first time since 1962. In 2006, the high-performance Z06 variant was added to the range and celebrated as the fastest and most powerful street-legal car ever released by General Motors. It gained a mammoth V8 with titanium connecting rods, aluminum and carbon fiber components to keep weight in check, and a wider track. It was replaced by the C7 in 2014. Power comes from a 7.0-liter V8. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 6-speed manual transmission.

Highlights: THIS... is a 1964 Porsche 356C Coupe, finished in Ruby Red with a black interior. This coupe has been partially restored, according to the selling dealer. A partial list of notable currently installed equipment reported by the selling dealer includes aftermarket 15-inch wheels, leatherette upholstery, and a Motorola AM radio. Released in 1948, the 356 was far from the first car designed by Ferdinand Porsche, but it was the first to wear the family name as a production model. Drawing heavily on his Volkswagen Type 1 — including its closely related air-cooled boxer four — the 356 combined sleek styling, nimble handling, and hand-built craftsmanship. Offered in a variety of body styles from coupe to roadster, it evolved steadily throughout its long production run, culminating in the 356C with standard four-wheel disc brakes. Power comes from a 1.6-liter air-cooled flat-4, rated at about 75 horsepower and 88 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 4-speed manual transmission.
Equipment: A partial list of notable currently installed equipment reported by the selling dealer includes: Aftermarket 15-inch wheels Headlight grilles Leatherette upholstery Motorola AM radio
Known Flaws: An invoice shown in the gallery notes that, due to breakage, the speedometer cable was replaced in May 2025. The odometer currently displays about 49,300 miles, but the true mileage is unknown. Scratches around the exterior Cracked window seals Blemishes on upholstery Discolored headliner

Highlights: THIS... is a 1984 Lincoln Continental Mark VII LSC Turbo Diesel, finished in Platinum Clearcoat Metallic with a Charcoal interior. The attached Carfax history report lists no accidents in this Mark VII's past. Factory equipment on this Mark VII includes 15-inch wheels, an air suspension system, fog lights, power-adjustable front seats, a digital instrument cluster, and a cassette player, according to a Marti Report pictured in the gallery. The sole modification reported by the seller is that the air conditioning system has been converted to R134a. Service records shown in the gallery detail some of the repairs and maintenance performed on this Mark VII during the seller's ownership. The engine oil and the engine oil filter were last changed at 46,027 miles in May 2021, and the wheels were balanced in March 2021. Launched for 1984, the Continental Mark VII represented Lincoln's attempt at adding a touch of European flair to its range in a bid to fend off Mercedes-Benz on its home turf. The Mark VII was shorter, lighter, and more streamlined than the model it replaced, and it was a lot more engaging to drive. The optional Luxury Sport Coupe (LSC) package brought a quicker steering system and a specific 3.27:1 axle ratio, among other changes. Lincoln was serious about taking on Mercedes-Benz. While most Mark VIIs came with a V8, buyers could optionally order a 2.4-liter "M21" turbodiesel 6-cylinder provided by BMW and shared with the 524td, among other models. Few did, only 2,334 buyers paid $1,235 (about $3,200 in 2021) for the turbodiesel engine in 1984, so the option disappeared for 1986. Power comes from a 2.4-liter turbodiesel 6-cylinder, rated at 115 horsepower and 155 lb-ft of torque. It spins the rear wheels via a 4-speed automatic transmission.
Equipment: 15-inch wheels Air suspension system Fog lights Keyless entry Cloth and leather upholstery Power-adjustable front seats Power-operated windows Heated and power-adjustable mirrors Digital instrument cluster Air conditioning Cassette player Premium sound system

Highlights: THIS… is a 2007 Jeep Wrangler Unlimited Sahara 4x4, finished in black with a Slate Gray interior. This Wrangler is equipped with a 6-speed manual transmission. The attached Carfax history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this Wrangler’s past. It also confirms that this is a two-owner Jeep registered in Washington since new. Introduced for 2007, the JK-generation was the first Wrangler offered with four doors from the factory. The lineup received a refreshed dash with a far more appealing design and increased sound-deadening materials for 2011. Jeep positioned the Sahara at the middle of the range and aimed it at enthusiasts and adventurers who wanted more features than the base Sport model, while still having room for modifications. Production of the JK ended in 2018. Power comes from a 3.8-liter V6, rated at 202 horsepower and 237 lb-ft of torque. Output is delivered to two or all four wheels through a 6-speed manual transmission and a two-speed "Rock-Trac" transfer case.

The model was named in Car and Driver magazine's "10Best" list every year it was on sale. It received a wider, longer, and generally sportier exterior than the E30 it replaced. Production ended after the 1999 model year when BMW phased in the E46. Power comes from a 1.8-liter inline-4, rated at 138 horsepower and 129 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels via a 5-speed manual transmission.

Highlights: THIS... is a 1996 Toyota HiAce Super Custom Living Saloon EX, finished in Super White and Oyster Pearl with a brown interior. This HiAce is a Japanese-spec, right-hand-drive van that's titled in Maryland. Imported from Japan in May 2022, it's equipped with a metric instrument cluster, and the odometer shows about 116,300 kilometers, which represents approximately 72,300 miles. Toyota introduced the fourth-generation HiAce (called "H100" internally) in 1989, and it sold the model in numerous global markets, though the United States wasn't one of them. Spacious and relatively compact thanks in part to its cab-over design, it was offered in people- and cargo-carrying variants until 2004. The Super Custom was a mid-range variant that masked its utilitarian DNA with a living room-like 8-seat interior. Power comes from a "1KZ-TE" 3.0-liter turbodiesel inline-4, rated at approximately 130 horsepower and 213 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to the rear wheels through a 4-speed automatic transmission.

Highlights: THIS... is a 2001 Subaru Impreza 2.5RS, finished in Aspen White with a black and blue interior. This Impreza is equipped with a 6-speed manual transmission. Modifications to this Impreza are extensive, and the full list is detailed below. Highlights include an EJ257 2.5-liter turbocharged flat-4 and 6-speed manual transmission from a 2005 STI, a carbon fiber 22B-style wide-body kit, aftermarket headlights, aftermarket fog lights, STI front and rear seats with matching door panels and rear side panels, an STI dash and gauge cluster, and an STI steering wheel, among others listed below. Subaru expanded the first-generation Impreza line-up with the 2.5RS model for the 1998 model year. Initially offered only as a coupe, it gained a more muscular exterior design characterized by round fog lights and a rear spoiler, sport seats for the front passengers, and a more powerful engine. In hindsight, Subaru brought the 2.5RS to America to determine whether it could make a favorable business case for selling the WRX here. Power comes from an EJ257 2.5-liter turbocharged flat-4, rated at 300 horsepower and 300 lb-ft of torque when left stock. The modifications increase those figures to approximately 319 horsepower and 338 lb-ft at the wheels, according to a dyno sheet pictured in the gallery. The Mk7 Golf was introduced in 2012 on the automaker's new modular MQB platform, although U.S.-market sales didn't begin until the 2015 model year. Praised as the quintessential hot hatch, it was bigger in every direction than its predecessor, so it offered a more spacious cabin, yet it was also about 80 pounds lighter. Highlights: THIS… is a 2004 Lexus SC 430 Pebble Beach Edition, finished in Azure Pearl with an Ecru interior. The odometer on this SC 430 currently displays approximately 47,400 miles. The attached Carfax history report shows no mileage discrepancies in this SC 430’s past. A build sheet is provided in the photo gallery, and a partial list of notable equipment includes the Pebble Beach Edition trim package, a power-operated hardtop, leather upholstery, power-adjustable and heated front seats, a navigation system, a 9-speaker Mark Levinson sound system, and more as detailed below. Modifications reported by the seller are limited to 18-inch Lexus alloy wheels. Lexus released the second-generation SC, its first series-produced convertible, for the 2001 model year. Much shorter and far more luxurious than its predecessor, it gained a more rounded design and came with a power-folding aluminum hardtop that let users switch between a hard-roofed coupe and an open-air convertible at the push of a button. The Pebble Beach Edition was an appearance package for the SC 430 that was offered in partnership with the Pebble Beach Company. In addition to a unique color combination that changed each year through 2009, Pebble Beach badging adorned the vehicle inside and out. This SC 430 was 1 of 400 Pebble Beach Editions produced for the 2004 model year. Power comes from a 3UZ-FE 4.3-liter V8, rated at 300 horsepower and 325 lb-ft of torque. Introduced to the American market for 2001, and produced until 2003, the "D2" S8 was a sportier evolution of the first-generation A8, which featured an all-aluminum platform named Audi Space Frame. Aimed directly at AMG's take on the Mercedes-Benz S-Class, the S8 stood out from the A8 with a more powerful V8, a lowered sport suspension system, speed-sensitive power steering, and electronic stability control. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 5-speed automatic transmission and Audi's Quattro permanent all-wheel drive system.

The flagship Type S, designed to elevate driving enthusiasts' experience, boasts a stiffer body structure, a turbocharged V6 engine, and Super Handling All-Wheel Drive (SH-AWD), delivering formidable performance and a thrilling driving experience. Acura's limited-run TLX Type S PMC Edition was offered in three eye-catching paint colors inspired by the NSX supercar: Curva Red, 130R White, and Long Beach Blue Pearl. Only 100 examples of each color were hand-built at Acura's Performance Manufacturing Center, and this is number 95 of 100 vehicles finished in Curva Red, according to a plaque on the center console. Power comes from a 3.0-liter turbocharged V6, rated at 355 horsepower and 354 lb-ft of torque. Output is sent to all four wheels via a 10-speed automatic transmission and Acura's Super Handling All-Wheel Drive (SH-AWD) system.

Highlights: THIS... is a 2003 Chevrolet Corvette Z06, finished in black with a black interior. This Corvette features a 6-speed manual transmission. The LS6 V8 adds 55 horsepower over the standard coupe, and an upgraded FE4 suspension completes the Z06's track-focused transformation. Complete with styling designed to pay homage to the Mercedes-Benz McLaren Formula One partnership of the time, the SLK55 represented an accessible entry point into the Mercedes-Benz AMG series of performance cars.

Many of the parts that set the GR Corolla apart from the hatchback it's based on are shared with the rally-bred GR Yaris sold overseas.
